message:
fix(mvcc): remove unnecessary locked_count() == 0 guard from capacity rebuild

The SharedPageLockTable previously required `active.locked_count() == 0`
before triggering a best-effort rebuild when the load factor exceeded
MAX_LOAD_FACTOR. This was unnecessarily conservative: the rolling rebuild
protocol is explicitly designed to handle active locks by migrating them
to the draining table during the rebuild. Requiring zero active locks
meant that under sustained concurrent load — where at least one lock is
nearly always held — historical key accumulation could push the table
past capacity and permanently block future lock acquisitions despite
plenty of released slots.

By removing the guard, we allow the rebuild to start immediately when
the load factor threshold is breached. The existing draining-table
migration logic handles any in-flight locks safely. This prevents lock
table exhaustion under high-contention MVCC workloads where transactions
overlap continuously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
